title: ACI Fault Domain Severity Rollup
spl:
index=cisco_aci sourcetype="cisco:aci:faults" earliest=-24h
| where severity IN ("critical","major") AND (isnull(lc) OR NOT match(lower(lc),"(?i)resolved|cleared"))
| stats dc(dn) as affected_objects, values(code) as codes by fault_domain, severity
| sort fault_domain, -affected_objects
| table fault_domain, severity, affected_objects, codes
description: Fault domains group infrastructure and policy failures by functional area (for example connectivity, configuration, or capacity). Rolling up open faults by domain shows where the fabric is structurally weak, helps prioritize remediation before east-west traffic degrades, and shortens war-room triage during incidents.

Rolling up open faults by domain shows where the fabric is structurally weak, helps prioritize remediation before east-west traffic degrades, and shortens war-room triage during incidents.
implementation: (1) Map `fault_domain` from APIC fault MO or TA extraction; if missing, derive from `dn` prefix via `rex`. (2) Schedule hourly and alert when any domain exceeds baseline affected object count. (3) Correlate spikes with change windows and interface faults (UC-18.1.6).
visualization: Stacked bar chart (faults by domain √ó severity), Table (top domains), Single value (open major+critical count).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: `TA_cisco-ACI`, Cisco DC Networking Application (Splunkbase 7777).
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=cisco_aci` `sourcetype="cisco:aci:faults"` with fields `fault_domain`, `severity`, `code`, `dn`, `lc`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
(1) Map `fault_domain` from APIC fault MO or TA extraction; if missing, derive from `dn` prefix via `rex`. (2) Schedule hourly and alert when any domain exceeds baseline affected object count. (3) Correlate spikes with change windows and interface faults (UC-18.1.6).

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cisco_aci sourcetype="cisco:aci:faults" earliest=-24h
| where severity IN ("critical","major") AND (isnull(lc) OR NOT match(lower(lc),"(?i)resolved|cleared"))
| stats dc(dn) as affected_objects, values(code) as codes by fault_domain, severity
| sort fault_domain, -affected_objects
| table fault_domain, severity, affected_objects, codes
```

Understanding this SPL

**ACI Fault Domain Severity Rollup** ‚Äî Fault domains group infrastructure and policy failures by functional area (for example connectivity, configuration, or capacity). Rolling up open faults by domain shows where the fabric is structurally weak, helps prioritize remediation before east-west traffic degrades, and shortens war-room triage during incidents.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=cisco_aci` `sourcetype="cisco:aci:faults"` with fields `fault_domain`, `severity`, `code`, `dn`, `lc`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `TA_cisco-ACI`, Cisco DC Networking Application (Splunkbase 7777). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: cisco_aci; **sourcetype**: cisco:aci:faults. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=cisco_aci, sourcetype="cisco:aci:faults", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where severity IN ("critical","major") AND (isnull(lc) OR NOT match(lower(lc),"(?i)resolved|cleared"))` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by fault_domain, severity** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
‚Ä¢ Pipeline stage (see **ACI Fault Domain Severity Rollup**): table fault_domain, severity, affected_objects, codes


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Compare with APIC fault browser and the affected object; verify clearing vs persistence on the same code and object DN.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Stacked bar chart (faults by domain √ó severity), Table (top domains), Single value (open major+critical count).
grandmaExplanation: Fault domains group infrastructure and policy failures by functional area (for example connectivity, configuration, or capacity) ‚Äî so traffic problems, routing mistakes, and risky changes surface fast.
knownFalsePositives: Faults during planned maintenance, hardware refresh, or after firmware upgrades.
